metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.grounding_notes: Grader's claims are supported by the RF_note.md text preview (values and calculations are present). The .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed (openpyxl/module missing) but that limitation is acknowledged in grader limitations. The actual_outputs key_results also mirror the RF_note.md values, providing additional confirmation.
extra_model_fields.hidden_reference_leakage_notes: Grader metadata indicates hidden_reference_leaked=false and hidden_reference_used=false; no evidence of hidden-reference leakage in previews or raw traces.
extra_model_fields.notes: Verifier concurs with grader that artifacts are present and the RF_note.md text preview supports the grading decisions. The primary limitation is inability to programmatically inspect the .xlsx; grader appropriately caveated this. No contradictions or leakage found.
extra_model_fields.reproducible_notes: Reproducibility is limited because the spreadsheet (.xlsx) could not be programmatically inspected in this environment (parse_status=openpyxl_unavailable). Reproduction would require an XLSX-capable runtime or a CSV/screenshot export of the workbook.
extra_model_fields.score_consistency_notes: Final_score and rubric item scores are internally consistent (all rubric items scored 1.0 and aggregated to final_score 1.0). Deterministic precheck also reports artifact_contract_score 1.0, matching the model grade.
extra_model_fields.unsupported_claims_notes: No unsupported factual claims detected. Numerical calculations (throughput/user) and rule applications (PRB>85, SINR<12 dB, throughput/user<2 Mbps) are documented in RF_note.md and align with actual_outputs.

extra_model_fields.grounding_notes: Grader's claims are supported by the RF_note.md text preview (values and calculations are present). The .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed (openpyxl/module missing) but that limitation is acknowledged in grader limitations. The actual_outputs key_results also mirror the RF_note.md values, providing additional confirmation.
extra_model_fields.hidden_reference_leakage_notes: Grader metadata indicates hidden_reference_leaked=false and hidden_reference_used=false; no evidence of hidden-reference leakage in previews or raw traces.
extra_model_fields.notes: Verifier concurs with grader that artifacts are present and the RF_note.md text preview supports the grading decisions. The primary limitation is inability to programmatically inspect the .xlsx; grader appropriately caveated this. No contradictions or leakage found.
extra_model_fields.reproducible_notes: Reproducibility is limited because the spreadsheet (.xlsx) could not be programmatically inspected in this environment (parse_status=openpyxl_unavailable). Reproduction would require an XLSX-capable runtime or a CSV/screenshot export of the workbook.
extra_model_fields.score_consistency_notes: Final_score and rubric item scores are internally consistent (all rubric items scored 1.0 and aggregated to final_score 1.0). Deterministic precheck also reports artifact_contract_score 1.0, matching the model grade.
extra_model_fields.unsupported_claims_notes: No unsupported factual claims detected. Numerical calculations (throughput/user) and rule applications (PRB>85, SINR<12 dB, throughput/user<2 Mbps) are documented in RF_note.md and align with actual_outputs.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: One minor issue: binary XLSX preview was unavailable (openpyxl missing) and the grader relied on normalized actual_outputs plus RF_note.md text preview. Otherwise evidence is grounded, scoring is internally consistent, no unsupported hidden-reference leakage detected.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

passed_checks: numeric values cross-checked between RF_note.md preview and actual_outputs_normalized

evidence_details: RF_note.md text preview is available and contains the numeric table and validation checks used by the grader.
Normalized actual_outputs contains structured spreadsheet values that match the RF_note.md table (PRB, SINR, users, throughput, throughput/user).
RAN_capacity_triage.xlsx binary preview failed (openpyxl_unavailable), but actual_outputs_normalized contains the same numeric values the grader cites.

hidden_reference_leakage.notes: Grader fields hidden_reference_leaked and hidden_reference_used are false and no evidence of hidden-reference content in outputs was found.

recommended_actions: If full end-to-end verification is required, enable openpyxl/xlsx parsing or provide an exported CSV/JSON of the spreadsheet to remove reliance on normalized actual_outputs.
Optionally include a checksum or explicit table export from the spreadsheet that the grader can parse directly to eliminate the binary-preview gap.
reliability_notes: Primary minor risk: inability to parse XLSX in preview environment means any deep binary-only discrepancies could be missed; however the normalized actual_outputs provides the canonical numeric values and matches the RF_note.md preview.

Deterministic precheck metadata documents that semantic correctness was not evaluated in the deterministic pass; the model grader subsequently performed semantic checks using RF_note.md and normalized actual_outputs.

score_consistency.notes: Final score 1.0 is supported by rubric item scores (all 1.0) and the stated aggregation weights resulting in final_weighted_sum 1.0.

unsupported_claims_or_misrepresentations.notes: Grader limitations and reliance on normalized actual_outputs due to openpyxl absence are explicitly declared in grader limitations; semantic grading performed against RF_note.md is supported by the provided preview and normalized actual_outputs.

raw_llm_output.verifier_issues_summary: One minor issue: binary XLSX preview was unavailable (openpyxl missing) and the grader relied on normalized actual_outputs plus RF_note.md text preview. Otherwise evidence is grounded, scoring is internally consistent, no unsupported hidden-reference leakage detected.
